facts.context: What is true for a type-Ia ("type one-a") supernova?
facts.truth: This type occurs in binary systems.
distractors: This type occurs in young galaxies.
This type produces gamma-ray bursts.
This type produces high amounts of X-rays.

facts.context: If you know both the actual brightness of an object and its apparent brightness from your location then with no other information you can estimate:
facts.truth: Its distance from you
distractors: Its speed relative to you
Its composition
Its size

facts.context: Why is the sky blue?
facts.truth: Because the atmosphere preferentially scatters short wavelengths.
distractors: Because the molecules that compose the Earth's atmosphere have a blue-ish color.
Because the sky reflects the color of the Earth's oceans.
Because the Earth's atmosphere preferentially absorbs all other colors.

facts.context: You’ve made a scientific theory that there is an attractive force between all objects. When will your theory be proven to be correct?
facts.truth: You can never prove your theory to be correct only “yet to be proven wrong”.
distractors: The first time you drop a bowling ball and it falls to the ground proving your hypothesis.
After you’ve repeated your experiment many times.
When you and many others have tested the hypothesis.

facts.context: Which of the following is/are true?
facts.truth: A and D
distractors: Titan is the only outer solar system moon with a thick atmosphere
Titan is the only outer solar system moon with evidence for recent geologic activity
Titan's atmosphere is composed mostly of hydrocarbons

facts.context: A comet of mass m impacts the earth (mass M radius R) at the minimum impact speed. What is the expression for the total energy released in the impact?
distractors: m*v

facts.context: Some of Mars'water is frozen in the soil and the ice caps and some was lost due to solar wind stripping but much of the original water is thought to have been lost through another mechanism. What happened to this "lost"water?
facts.truth: it was broken into hydrogen and oxygen by ultraviolet light and the hydrogen was lost through thermal escape
distractors: it was stripped away by the magnetic field when it rapidly decreased in strength
it was consumed by a civilization of thirsty Martians
it was vaporized during a period of intense volcanism and was lost to space through thermal escape

facts.context: Most rocks on the Moon's surface are older than those on the Earth's surface. The best evidence for this is:
facts.truth: Radioactive dating of lunar samples shows that they are older.
distractors: Lunar rocks are composed of fragments pulverized by many impacts.
The Moon's surface is more heavily eroded than the Earth's surface.
The Moon's surface has more impact craters than the Earth's surface.

facts.context: What is the second most common element in the solar system?
facts.truth: Helium
distractors: Iron
Hydrogen
Methane

facts.context: What is the source of the material that causes meteor showers?
facts.truth: The nuclei of comets gradually disintegrate and spread out along the comet's orbital path. When the Earth passes through the orbit of a comet we are bombarded by sand-sized particles which cause a meteor shower.
distractors: Near-Earth asteroids gradually disintegrate and spread out along their orbital path. When the Earth passes through the orbit of an asteroid we are bombarded by sand-sized particles which cause a meteor shower.
Near-Earth asteroids disintegrate as they enter Earth's atmosphere creating hundreds of bright meteors that appear to radiate from a single location in the sky.
The nuclei of comets disintigrate as they enter Earth's atmosphere creating hundreds of bright meteors that appear to radiate from a central location in the sky.

facts.context: Why does the plasma (ion) tail of a comet always point away from the Sun?
facts.truth: The solar wind electromagnetically "blows" the ions directly away from the Sun.
distractors: Radiation pressure from the Sun's light pushes the ions away.
The conservation of the angular momentum in the tail keeps it always pointing away from the Sun.
The ions are following Keplerian orbits that lead away from the Sun.

facts.context: How did Eratosthenes estimate the size of Earth in 240 B.C.?
facts.truth: By comparing the maximum altitude of the Sun in two cities at different latitudes at the same time on the same day.
distractors: By observing the duration of a solar eclipse.
By measuring the size of Earth's shadow on the Moon in a lunar eclipse.
By sending fleets of ships around Earth.

facts.context: The Large Magellanic Cloud is ...
facts.truth: a dwarf galaxy orbiting the Milky Way.
distractors: the closest planetary nebula to the Earth.
a bright star cluster discovered by Magellan.
the outer arm of the Milky Way named after Magellan.

facts.context: Why do we look for water-ice in craters at Mercury's pole?
facts.truth: These craters contain the only permanently shadowed regions on Mercury
distractors: Actually water-ice is all over Mercury and not just at the poles.
The pole is the only place fortunate enough to have had comet impacts
Radar from the earth can only see Mercury's poles.

facts.context: The Milky Way is part of a giant supercluster with a diameter of 160 Mpc. What is the name of this supercluster?
facts.truth: Laniakea
distractors: Virgo
Sculptor
Boötes

facts.context: On which planet in our solar system can you find the Great Red Spot?
facts.truth: Jupiter
distractors: Venus
Mars
Saturn

facts.context: Why does Earth have the strongest magnetic field among the terrestrial worlds?
facts.truth: It is the only one that has both a partially molten metallic core and reasonably rapid rotation.
distractors: It is by far the largest terrestrial world.
It is the most volcanically active world.
It rotates much faster than any other terrestrial world.

facts.context: Which of the following is not true?
facts.truth: The Earth and Venus are in a 1:1 orbital resonance (for every 1 Earth orbit there is exactly 1 Venus orbit)
distractors: Neptune and Pluto are in a 3:2 orbital resonance (for every 3 Neptune orbits there are exactly 2 Pluto orbits)
The Kirkwood Gaps in the asteroid belt are due to resonances with Jupiter
Neptune and Pluto won't collide because of their orbital resonance

facts.context: Which of the following is/are common feature(s) of all fresh (i.e. not eroded) impact craters formed on solid surfaces:
facts.truth: A and B only
distractors: ejecta
raised rims
central peaks

facts.context: From laboratory measurements we know that a particular spectral line formed by hydrogen appears at a wavelength of 486.1 nanometers (nm). The spectrum of a particular star shows the same hydrogen line appearing at a wavelength of 485.9 nm. What can we conclude?
facts.truth: The star is moving toward us.
distractors: The star is getting hotter.
The star is getting colder.
The star is moving away from us.

facts.context: The lithosphere of a planet is the layer that consists of
facts.truth: the rigid rocky material of the crust and uppermost portion of the mantle.
distractors: the softer rocky material of the mantle.
the lava that comes out of volcanoes.
material between the crust and the mantle.

facts.context: What is the Cassini division of Saturn's rings?
facts.truth: a large gap visible from Earth produced by an orbital resonance with the moon Mimas
distractors: a dark ring visible from Earth composed of dark dusty particles
the most opaque ring of Saturn made of highly reflective ice particles
the widest ring of Saturn located between two large ring gaps

facts.context: Which of the following statements about sidereal and solar days is not true?
facts.truth: The time it takes for the Moon to make one circuit of our sky is one solar day.
distractors: The time it takes for the Sun to make one circuit of our sky is one solar day.
A solar day is 4 minutes longer than a sidereal day.
A solar day represents more than 360° of rotation for the earth.

facts.context: Meteorites with high metal content probably are
facts.truth: chunks of large differentiated asteroids that were shattered by collisions.
distractors: pieces of comets rather than of asteroids.
chunks of rock chipped off the planet Mars.
leftover chunks of rock from the earliest period in the formation of the solar system.

facts.context: Most people use C°(degree Celsius) to measure everyday temperatures. Astronomers prefer to use K (Kelvin) to measure temperatures. Approximately how many Kelvin are 25 C°?
facts.truth: 300 Kelvin
distractors: 200 Kelvin
250 Kelvin
350 Kelvin

facts.context: The four big moons of Jupiter are Callisto Europa Ganymede and Io. Which one of them has the smallest distance to Jupiter?
facts.truth: Io
distractors: Callisto
Europa
Ganymede

facts.context: Why are the season's in Mars' southern hemisphere so extreme?
facts.truth: because Mars has a more eccentric orbit than the Earth
distractors: because Mars is farther from the sun than the Earth
because Mars has more carbon dioxide in its atmosphere than the Earth
because Mars' axis is more tilted than the Earth's

facts.context: What Mars mission will be landing on May 25 2008 and will dig a trench into (hopefully) ice-rich soil?
facts.truth: Phoenix Mars Lander
distractors: ExoMars

Mars Science Laboratory

facts.context: What is the significance of the 1:2:4 resonance in the Jupiter's moons system?
facts.truth: It makes the orbit of Io slightly elliptical.
distractors: The resonance pulls Io in different directions and generates heat.
It creates a gap with no asteriods between the orbits.
It prevents formation of the ring material into other moons.

facts.context: The terrestrial planet cores contain mostly metal because
facts.truth: metals sank to the center during a time when the interiors were molten throughout.
distractors: the entire planets are made mostly of metal.
metals condensed first in the solar nebula and the rocks then accreted around them.
radioactivity created metals in the core from the decay of uranium.

facts.context: Why are the inner planets made of denser materials than the outer planets?
facts.truth: In the inner part of the nebula only metals and rocks were able to condense because of the high temperatures whereas hydrogen compounds although more abundant were only able to condense in the cooler outer regions.
distractors: In the beginning when the protoplanetary disk was spinning faster centrifugal forces flung the lighter materials toward the outer parts of the solar nebula.
Denser materials were heavier and sank to the center of the nebula.

When the solar nebula formed a disk materials naturally segregated into bands and in our particular solar system the denser materials settled nearer the Sun while lighter materials are found in the outer part.

facts.context: What do meteorites reveal about the solar system?
facts.truth: They reveal that the age of the solar system is approximately 4.6 billion years.
distractors: They reveal that the early solar system consisted mostly of hydrogen and helium gas.
They reveal that meteorites are much older than the comets and planets.
They reveal that the solar system once contained 10 planets.

facts.context: Venus shows evidence of which of the following surface processes?
facts.truth: A B and C
distractors: Impacts
Erosion
Volcanism

facts.context: Planetary rings are
facts.truth: all of the above
distractors: known to exist for all of the jovian planets.
composed of a large number of individual particles that orbit their planet in accord with Kepler's third law.
nearer to their planet than any of the planet's large moons.

facts.context: Suppose the angular separation of two stars is smaller than the angular resolution of your eyes. How will the stars appear to your eyes?
facts.truth: The two stars will look like a single point of light.
distractors: You will not be able to see these two stars at all.
You will see two distinct stars.
The two stars will appear to be touching looking rather like a small dumbbell.

facts.context: What is not true of Jupiter's magnetic field?
facts.truth: it switches polarity every 11 years
distractors: it is about 20000 times stronger than Earth's magnetic field
it traps charged particles from Io's volcanoes in a "plasma torus" around the planet
it causes spectacular auroral displays at Jupiter's north and south poles

facts.context: What does the astronomical term ecliptic describe?
facts.truth: The path of the Sun in the sky throughout a year.
distractors: The axial tilt of the Earth throughout a year.

facts.context: The so-called dark energy is a model to explain ...
facts.truth: the acceleration of the universe.
distractors: the radiation of black holes.
the mass distribution of galaxies.
the microwave background of the universe.

facts.context: How do scientists know that the majority of meteorites come from the asteroid belt?
facts.truth: The spectra of some meteorites are similar to the spectra of asteroids in the asteroid belt.
distractors: Bubbles of gas trapped in the crystals within meteorites are identical to the gases trapped in asteroids.
Collisions are common in the asteroid belt and we can track the fragments from their source asteroid to the Earth where they become meteorites.
The asteroid belt is the only possible source of meteorites therefore they must originate there.

facts.context: Jupiter and the other jovian planets are sometimes called "gas giants." In what sense is this term misleading?
facts.truth: They actually contain a significant fraction of non-gaseous matter.
distractors: The materials they are made of are not normally gaseous in everyday experience.
The materials that make up these planets are primarily in the form of a plasma not a gas.
Actually it's a great description because these worlds are big and gaseous throughout.

facts.context: Which of the following is not one of nor follows directly from Kepler's laws?
facts.truth: The force of attraction between any two objects decreases with the square of the distance between their centers.
distractors: As a planet moves around its orbit it sweeps out equal areas in equal times.
The orbit of each planet about the Sun is an ellipse with the Sun at one focus.
A planet travels faster when it is nearer to the Sun and slower when it is farther from the Sun.

facts.context: Why is Saturn almost as big as Jupiter despite its smaller mass?

distractors: Saturn has a larger proportion of hydrogen and helium than Jupiter and is therefore less dense.
Jupiter's strong magnetic field constrains its size.
Saturn is further from the Sun thus cooler and therefore less compact.

facts.context: Which of these has NOT been one of the main hypotheses considered for the origin of the Moon?
facts.truth: The Moon split from the Earth due to tidal forces.
distractors: The Moon was captured into Earth orbit.
The Earth and Moon co-accreted in the solar nebula.

facts.context: According to the Solar Nebular theory what are asteroids and comets?
facts.truth: They are leftover planetesimals that never accreted into planets.
distractors: They are the shattered remains of collisions between planets.

facts.context: How do we know how old the Earth is?
facts.truth: From radioactive dating of rocks and meteorites.
distractors: From the layering of materials within the Earth.

facts.truth: We don't know for sure.

comets.

facts.context: How does the greenhouse effect work?
facts.truth: Greenhouse gases transmit visible light allowing it to heat the surface but then absorb infrared light from Earth trapping the heat near the surface.
distractors: Ozone transmits visible light allowing it to heat the surface but then absorbs most of the infrared heat trapping the heat near the surface.
Greenhouse gases absorb infrared light from the Sun which then heats the atmosphere and the surface.
Greenhouse gases absorb X-rays and ultraviolet light from the Sun which then heat the atmosphere and the surface.

facts.context: Why is Saturn almost as big as Jupiter despite its smaller mass?
facts.truth: Jupiter's greater mass compresses it more thus increasing its density.
distractors: Saturn has a larger proportion of hydrogen and helium than Jupiter and is therefore less dense.
Saturn is further from the Sun thus cooler and therefore less compact.
Saturn's rings make the planet look bigger.

distractors: without a strong magnetic field the ozone layer was destroyed by the solar wind which stopped the greenhouse effect and cooled Mars down.
without a strong magnetic field which enhances the greenhouse effect Mars cooled down.
without a strong magnetic field the planet recieves fewer charged particles from the solar wind and therefore is not heated as much

facts.context: What is the source of the material that causes meteor showers?
facts.truth: The nuclei of comets gradually disintegrate and spread out along their orbital paths. When the Earth passes through the orbit of an comet we are bombarded by sand-sized particles that cause a meteor shower.
distractors: Asteroid impacts elsewhere in the solar system throw sand-sized particles into space and occasionally the Earth passes through a cloud of these particles which burn up in our atmosphere and cause a meteor shower.

Near-Earth asteroids gradually disintegrate and spread out along their orbital paths. When the Earth passes through the orbit of an asteroid we are bombarded by sand-sized particles that cause a meteor shower.
Near-Earth asteroids disintegrate as they enter Earth's atmosphere creating hundreds of bright meteors that appear to radiate from a single location in the sky.

facts.context: How does the greenhouse effect work?
facts.truth: Greenhouse gases transmit visible light allowing it to heat the surface but then absorb infrared light from Earth trapping the heat near the surface.
distractors: Greenhouse gases absorb infrared light from the Sun which then heats the atmosphere and the surface.
Ozone transmits visible light allowing it to heat the surface but then absorbs most of the infrared heat trapping the heat near the surface.
The higher pressure of the thick atmosphere at lower altitudes traps heat in more effectively.
